bugs_found.description: When a cache entry is expired (expires < time.time()), the code still returns the value instead of returning None. The expired entry is never deleted.
bugs_found.fix: Delete the expired entry from the store and return None.

bugs_found.description: _build_headers assigns self.default_headers directly to a local variable, then mutates it with update(). This permanently modifies the default headers dict.
bugs_found.fix: Create a copy with dict(self.default_headers) before merging extra headers.

bugs_found.description: api.py uses json.loads() but does not import the json module.
bugs_found.fix: Added 'import json' at the top of the file.

bugs_found.description: list_resources() does not pass authentication headers to client.get(), unlike get_resource() and create_resource().
bugs_found.fix: Added headers=self._get_auth_headers() to the client.get() call.

interaction_analysis: The bugs interact across files in several ways: (1) cache.py returning expired entries means client.py serves stale data that api.py then parses, potentially returning outdated resource data. (2) client.py's header mutation bug means that after the first request with auth headers in api.py, ALL subsequent requests include the Authorization header even when not intended, because self.default_headers is permanently modified. (3) The missing json import in api.py only manifests at runtime when parsing responses from client.py. (4) The missing auth headers in list_resources would cause 401 errors from the server while other endpoints work correctly.
